name: L-Ascorbic Acid (LAA)
description: The gold standard of Vitamin C. L-Ascorbic Acid is the most researched and bioavailable form, but also the most unstable. It requires a low pH (below 3.5) to penetrate the skin and work effectively. Best stored in dark, cool conditions and used within 3 months of opening.
science_deep_dive: LAA acts as the active cofactor for collagen-synthesizing enzymes prolyl and lysyl hydroxylase. It also inhibits tyrosinase — the enzyme responsible for melanin production — making it effective for both anti-aging and brightening.
stability_note: Oxidizes to dehydroascorbic acid (DHAA) and then to diketogulonic acid (DKA). An orange/brown tint indicates oxidation.

name: Ethyl Ascorbic Acid (EAA)
description: The best all-rounder Vitamin C derivative for Indian skin. EAA offers exceptional stability even in warm conditions, making it ideal for a country like India where temperatures and humidity are high. It converts to pure Vitamin C upon absorption.
science_deep_dive: The ethyl group at the 3-position of Ascorbic Acid's ring structure protects it from oxidation, while maintaining the ability to be hydrolyzed back to LAA by intracellular enzymes. Bioavailability is nearly equivalent to pure LAA.
stability_note: Highly stable — does not require refrigeration. Does not oxidize or change color during shelf life.

name: Sodium Ascorbyl Phosphate (SAP)
description: The dermatologist's choice for acne-prone skin. SAP has an added antibacterial property that makes it unique among Vitamin C derivatives — it actually helps fight Cutibacterium acnes (the acne-causing bacteria) while brightening skin simultaneously.
science_deep_dive: SAP is converted to LAA by phosphatase enzymes in the skin. Its antibacterial mechanism works via photo-excitation — when exposed to light, SAP generates reactive oxygen species that destroy bacterial cell membranes.
stability_note: Very stable in aqueous formula. The sodium salt form prevents oxidation at neutral pH levels.

name: Magnesium Ascorbyl Phosphate (MAP)
description: The hydration hero of Vitamin C derivatives. MAP is a water-binding form of Vitamin C that uniquely moisturizes the skin while brightening. It's the gentlest option available and works beautifully for dry and mature skin types.
science_deep_dive: The magnesium salt form creates a hygroscopic molecule that attracts water to the skin. When converted to LAA by phosphatase enzymes, it provides vitamin C's antioxidant and melanin-inhibiting effects.
stability_note: Stable at neutral pH. Requires proper formulation to avoid hydrolysis issues.

name: Ascorbyl Glucoside (AA2G™)
description: The most stable Vitamin C derivative available. AA2G is formed by bonding Ascorbic Acid with glucose, creating an incredibly stable molecule that resists oxidation under virtually all conditions. The tradeoff is lower bioavailability, requiring enzymatic cleavage by skin glucosidases.
science_deep_dive: The glucose moiety protects the ascorbic acid molecule from oxidation completely. Skin glucosidases slowly cleave the glucose bond over time, releasing LAA gradually. This sustained-release effect makes it excellent for long-term glow maintenance.
stability_note: Essentially oxidation-proof. No color change or degradation even over prolonged shelf life.
